topics.title: AI Infrastructure
topics.executive_summary: NVIDIA unveiled its Rubin generation platform featuring six new chips and new GPU architectures, while simultaneously announcing major strategic partnerships with Meta, Intel, and cloud providers to deploy millions of next-generation accelerators across hyperscale infrastructure.

stories.headline: NVIDIA Rubin: Six-chip generation unleashes next-gen AI supercomputers
stories.body: <cite index="1-3,1-4">NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 combines 72 Rubin GPUs, 36 Vera CPUs, and NVLink 6, while the HGX Rubin NVL8 platform links eight Rubin GPUs through NVLink</cite>. <cite index="1-1">BlueField-4 introduces ASTRA, a system-level trust architecture for securely provisioning large-scale AI environments</cite>.

stories.headline: Meta deploys millions of Blackwell and Rubin GPUs with NVIDIA
stories.body: <cite index="3-2,3-6,3-7">NVIDIA announced a multiyear, multigenerational strategic partnership with Meta spanning on-premises, cloud and AI infrastructure, with Meta building hyperscale data centers optimized for both training and inference</cite>. <cite index="3-3,3-4">Meta expands NVIDIA CPU deployment and uses NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet to scale AI workloads</cite>.

stories.headline: NVIDIA and Intel forge custom AI chip collaboration
stories.body: <cite index="6-1,6-4">Intel will build NVIDIA-custom x86 CPUs that NVIDIA will integrate into its AI infrastructure platforms</cite>. <cite index="6-3">The companies will connect NVIDIA and Intel architectures using NVIDIA NVLink, integrating NVIDIA's AI and accelerated computing with Intel's CPU technologies</cite>.
